name: Admin permission audit report command (/auditoria_permisos)
risks:
1) DATA DEPENDENCY, NOT YET POPULATED: this command reads `AdminDecisionRepository.listRecent()` (packages/data/src/admin-decision-repository.ts) for lastActionMs, and `KnownAdminRepository.listKnownAdmins()` (packages/data/src/known-admin-repository.ts) for a precise canPromote signal. BOTH files already exist in the tree (written by sibling in-flight workstreams: "aggressive-admin-consistency" and "external-admin audit" respectively) but neither is wired to a live `.record()`/`.addKnownAdmin()` call site anywhere in bot-update.service.ts yet, and BOTH reference Prisma models (AdminDecision, KnownAdmin) that do not exist in schema.prisma yet either (confirmed: `node node_modules/typescript/bin/tsc -p packages/data/tsconfig.json --noEmit` currently fails on admin-decision-repository.ts and known-admin-repository.ts for exactly this reason — a pre-existing baseline issue, not something I introduced). Practical effect until those two things land: `listRecent`/`listKnownAdmins` return empty for every chat, so every admin holding a dangerous permission (which today is assumed to be every non-owner admin, see point 2) will show up as "sin actividad reciente" — i.e. the report over-flags rather than under-flags, which is the safe direction for an audit-only command, but it means the report isn't actually discriminating until (a) schema.prisma gets the AdminDecision + KnownAdmin models and (b) something calls `adminDecisionRepository.record(...)` at the moderation-apply sites and `knownAdminRepository.addKnownAdmin/removeKnownAdmin` at the /promote and /demote sites. I did not add those calls myself since bot-update.service.ts is off-limits for direct edits and wiring 6+ call sites felt like scope creep onto the sibling workstream that already owns admin-decision-repository.ts for its own two commands — flagging this clearly instead so whoever integrates can decide whether to wire it now or ship the command dormant-but-safe until the sibling work lands.
2) GATEWAY GAP (as the task anticipated): `TelegramChatAdminInfo` (packages/telegram/src/gateway.ts, lines 111-117) exposes only `userId, username, firstName, isOwner, customTitle` — it does NOT expose Telegram's real per-admin permission booleans (can_restrict_members / can_delete_messages / can_pin_messages / can_promote_members), even though the raw getChatAdministrators response Telegram sends back does carry them; gateway.ts's parsing code (lines ~1086-1124) simply discards them. I did not edit gateway.ts. Instead I approximate: canBan/canDelete/canPin default to `true` for every admin (over-flagging is the safe direction for an audit-only report) and canPromote is derived more precisely from KnownAdminRepository — see integration notes — but that's still an approximation for admins promoted directly in Telegram (outside this bot), since we have no visibility into their real grant. A real fix requires extending gateway.ts's getChatAdministrators to parse `can_restrict_members`/`can_delete_messages`/`can_pin_messages`/`can_promote_members` off the raw entry into TelegramChatAdminInfo — flagging for whoever owns gateway.ts, not doing it myself per the task's instruction.
3) Two new DI tokens + two new provider registrations are needed (apps/bot/src/tokens.ts, apps/bot/src/app.module.ts) — both files are outside the three explicitly forbidden ones but are evidently mid-edit by other processes this session (both show as modified in git status), so I only describe the exact lines to add in botUpdateServiceIntegrationNotes rather than editing them, to avoid clobbering concurrent work.
4) No automatic revocation anywhere — this is a pure read/report command, consistent with the task's "audit only" requirement.
botUpdateServiceIntegrationNotes:
WHERE EACH PIECE GOES (all line numbers are current-as-read; re-locate by content if the file has moved since):

1. Type-only `@superbot/data` import block spans lines 5-63 (ends `} from "@superbot/data";`). Add `type AdminDecisionRepository,` and `type KnownAdminRepository,` anywhere in it — both are already defined and exported (packages/data/src/admin-decision-repository.ts and known-admin-repository.ts are both already in the tree and already re-exported from packages/data/src/index.ts, lines 48-49 and 52-53 respectively; I did not touch either file or index.ts, since they already existed).

2. Value `@superbot/data` import block spans lines 64-89 (ends `} from "@superbot/data";`). Add `InMemoryAdminDecisionRepository,` and `InMemoryKnownAdminRepository,`.

3. `@superbot/module-security` import block spans lines 390-515 (ends `} from "@superbot/module-security";`). Add `findExcessPermissions,` and `type AdminPerms,` — both already exported from modules/security/src/index.ts (line 85 exports `./permission-audit.js`), so no changes needed to that package either.

4. apps/bot/src/tokens.ts: append `ADMIN_DECISION_REPOSITORY` and `KNOWN_ADMIN_REPOSITORY` Symbol constants, following the exact one-liner pattern every other token uses (see tokens.ts lines 1-56). Then import both into bot-update.service.ts's `from "./tokens.js"` block (lines 621-672, alphabetical, formatter will resort).

5. apps/bot/src/app.module.ts: add two provider entries next to the existing ones (see lines 300-322 for the pattern, e.g. right after the `GUARDIAN_REPOSITORY` entry at lines 319-322):
   { provide: ADMIN_DECISION_REPOSITORY, useFactory: () => new PrismaAdminDecisionRepository() },
   { provide: KNOWN_ADMIN_REPOSITORY, useFactory: () => new PrismaKnownAdminRepository() },
   (both classes already exist and are exported; import them + the two new token symbols into app.module.ts's existing import blocks the same way GUARDIAN_REPOSITORY/PrismaGuardianRepository are imported there.)

6. Constructor: add the two new `@Optional() @Inject(...) private readonly ... = new InMemory...()` params right before the closing `) {}` of the BotUpdateService constructor (currently line 902), following the exact style of every other repository param in that constructor (e.g. `chatActivityRepository` at lines 894-896, `guardianRepository` at lines 900-901).

7. New private method: place it anywhere among the other `private async handleXCommand(...)` methods — I'd suggest right after `handleAdminToolCommand` (which currently runs from line 14878 to ~15050), since both are admin-facing chat-tool commands and share the `isGroupAdmin` gating idiom.

8. botHandlers() registration: add the new entry object to the array built in `botHandlers()` (lines 1116+); I placed it right after the existing `"admin-tool.command"` entry (lines 1561-1565) since it's topically adjacent, but position within the array doesn't affect dispatch order for a distinct command name.

9. OPTIONAL follow-up (not included in the snippet above, for whoever wires it): to make `knownAdminRepository` actually populate, add `await this.knownAdminRepository.addKnownAdmin(context.tenantId, context.chatId, command.userId)` in the `case "promote":` branch of `handleAdminToolCommand` (around line 14991-15001, right after a successful `promoteChatMember` call) and `await this.knownAdminRepository.removeKnownAdmin(...)` in the `case "demote":` branch (around line 15003-15012). To make `adminDecisionRepository` actually populate, add `await this.adminDecisionRepository.record({ tenantId: context.tenantId, chatId: context.chatId, adminId: update.user.userId ?? 0n, action: <ban|mute|kick|warn|...> })` alongside the existing `this.repository.recordAudit(...)` calls at the moderation-apply site (~lines 3139, 3245-3258, 8161) and the admin-tool site (~line 15039-15047). I did not add these myself: they touch many call sites inside the very file I'm not allowed to edit directly, and admin-decision-repository.ts's own doc comment already frames it as feeding "aggressive-admin.ts" and "consistency-check.ts" (a separate sibling workstream that most likely owns wiring these same call sites for its own two commands) — duplicating that wiring here risked a conflicting/duplicate edit. Until one of the two lands, /auditoria_permisos still runs safely, it just won't have a meaningfully discriminating "recent activity" signal yet (see risks).

10. Verification performed: `node node_modules/typescript/bin/tsc -p packages/data/tsconfig.json --noEmit` was run before touching anything — it already fails today on `admin-decision-repository.ts` and `known-admin-repository.ts` (both reference Prisma models that don't exist in schema.prisma yet: `AdminDecision`, `KnownAdmin`). This is a pre-existing state from the sibling workstreams, not something introduced by this task — I made no repository file changes of my own (see risks #1), so there is nothing new of mine to typecheck or test in packages/data.
